If you’re interested in the details of how we got it working, read on. Once a month, the Engineering team has a Hack Day. Anybody can work on anything they like, be it discovering a new programming language,…

title: Bulk Loading Multiple Tables
summary: AdRoll’s customer dashboard is powered by our HBase cluster, populated using both Storm and Hadoop MapReduce. Using Storm allows us to provide real-time statistics to our users, while Hadoop gives us the accuracy guarantees needed for billing. Throughout the day, Storm emits a steady stream of writes to HBase. Our MapReduce jobs however, run once a day over the previous…

title: Hadoop at AdRoll
summary: Earlier this week, AdRoll hosted a group of Hadoop users and developers for their monthly meetup. A member of our data team presented AdRoll’s infrastructure and described how we combine Hadoop with Storm and HBase to achieve both high accuracy and low latency. Here are the slides from that talk.

title: Valentino Volonghi Presents AdRoll's RTB Infrastructure at AWS Summit 2014
summary: Continuing with our video posts, here is Valentino once again, this time presenting at the 2014 AWS Summit in San Francisco. Valentino discusses how to handle 50 billion requests per day without breaking the bank by optimizing our AWS operations. In addition to low cost, AWS allows AdRoll’s RTB infrastructure to achieve nearly 100% uptime with sub-100ms latency and less…

title: Werner Vogels' Fireside Chat with Valentino
summary: Better late than never, but we wanted to share a video from last fall’s AWS re:Invent conference where our own chief architect Valentino Volonghi shared the stage with Amazon CTO Werner Vogels for a fireside chat. title: How to add JSCS to your pre-commit hook
summary: I recently came across JSCS and thought it would be a great addition to our git flow to enforce consistent code style for all of our JavaScript code. title: Lazy loading Backbone collections with Promises

title: Multi-Region DynamoDB
summary: In rolling out our real time bidding infrastructure, we were faced with the task of syncing data for every user we could possibly target across four regions. title: DNS-less setup in AWS
summary: Anybody that has worked with AWS must deal with the arbitrary hostnames that AWS generates for each instance. These names are difficult to remember and not particularly useful to understand what tasks any particular node is assigned to. The classic solution to this problem is to use a DNS server, but unfortunately setting up and maintaining a DNS system can…

title: HyperLogLog and MinHash
